action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 19 achieved only 77.12% average accuracy, far below the best of 83.11% from iteration 10. The recent AdamW attempts (iterations 15-19) all reset the linear head at the start of each task, while the successful 82.43% run (iteration 8) likely used a persistent head. Reinitializing the head destroys old-task decision boundaries and, with only 20-30 epochs per task, the optimizer fails to fully reconverge on the growing buffer (45k samples, 100 classes). Analysis: Iteration 8's training curves show smooth monotonic increases without catastrophic drops at task boundaries, consistent with a persistent head. The LBFGS run (iter 18, 81.89%) also reinitialized the head and fell short. I will revert to the proven recipe: frozen IN21k ViT-B/16 + cosine linear head + full feature replay + persistent head (NO reset per task) + AdamW + cosine annealing + label smoothing + feature noise. The head is never reset; instead the optimizer state is refreshed each task to adapt to new data while preserving learned boundaries. This should recover the ~82-83% baseline. Decision: Rewrite train.py to remove head reset, keep all other hyperparameters identical to the clean iter-19 setup, run 30 epochs per task, verify predict.py, and submit eval.
